Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: suit coats [300434093], coats (garments) [300046143], main garments [300209263], clothing [300266639], costume (mode of fashion) [300178802], Costume (hierarchy name) [300209261], Furnishings and Equipment (hierarchy name) [300264551], Objects Facet [300264092]

Non-Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: jackets (short coats) [300046167], main garments for the upper body [300209278], main garments [300209263], clothing [300266639], costume (mode of fashion) [300178802], Costume (hierarchy name) [300209261], Furnishings and Equipment (hierarchy name) [300264551], Objects Facet [300264092]

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Men's suit coats cut sack-style, usually with satin- or faille-faced lapels. Worn with trousers having a stripe down the outside of the leg to form a tuxedo.

Source.Source_ID: 2000030201/National Museum of American History, Dictionary of Costume (n.d.)

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Traje de chaqueta masculino cortado con el estilo del saco; generalmente, con solapas de raso o falla. Se lleva con pantalones y tiene una cinta que desciende hacia la parte externa de la pierna para formar un esmoquin.

Jasjes die onderdeel uitmaken van een 'smoking', zonder taille, meestal met satijnen of faille revers. Gedragen met een broek met bies die langs de buitennaad van de pijpen loopt om een smoking te vormen. Zie 'smokings' en 'jasjes'.
Untaillierte Anzugsjacke für Herren, in der Regel mit einem Revers aus Satin oder Taft. Bildet mit einer Hose, die einen Streifen an der Außenseite des Beins besitzt, einen Smoking.

Source.Source_ID: 2000046682/Random House Dictionary of the English Language (1987)
2000106041/Centraal Museum Utrecht, MoMu Fashion Museum of Antwerp databases (2018-)
2000106041/Centraal Museum Utrecht, MoMu Fashion Museum of Antwerp databases (2018-)
2000106166/Nomenclature for Museum Cataloging / Nomenclature pour le catalogage translation project (2016-)

Term_Source.Page: Term suggested by Valerie Mendes and Avril Hart, V & A Residency 3/93.

Source.Source_ID: 2000030201/National Museum of American History, Dictionary of Costume (n.d.)
